intro: Alpinia officinarum, also known as lesser galangal, is a type of rhizome commonly used in traditional medicine and as a culinary spice in Southeast Asian cuisine. The powder made from its dried and ground rhizome is often used to treat digestive issues, colds, and respiratory problems due to its purported antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. However, its effects and safety profile as a dietary supplement or medicinal ingredient have not been extensively scientifically studied or validated.
